mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.4 percentage points within a single month, it signals a sudden labor market deterioration that triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of real personal savings (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates a synchronized pullback in consumer liquidity and durable goods demand, which compresses corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression of 20-day SPX returns, or the joint signal fails to outperform a random baseline in 500 bootstrap samples.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of tight capacity utilization, a sustained increase in the industrial production index (INDPRO) expands corporate profit margins by lowering unit costs, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as earnings catch up to supply growth.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns show no positive correlation with INDPRO growth when capacity utilization exceeds 85%, or the signal's information ratio falls below 0.2 over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the federal funds rate exceeding 0.25 percentage points forces immediate repricing of duration risk, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as leveraged equity positions unwind.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero following rate hikes, or the signal generates a negative Sharpe ratio over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A continuous month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects robust labor demand that supports aggregate consumption and corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as earnings momentum builds.
failure_condition: The rolling 60-day correlation between payroll growth and SPX returns turns negative for two consecutive quarters, or the signal's hit rate drops below 45% in a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous acceleration of real GDP growth (GDPC1) and personal savings (RSAFS) signals a healthy, liquidity-backed consumption boom that lifts equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The cross-product term of GDPC1 and RSAFS growth rates shows no predictive power for 20-day SPX returns, or the interaction signal underperforms a single-proxy baseline in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) declines while the unemployment rate (UNRATE) remains below 5.0%, it indicates a temporary supply-side bottleneck rather than a demand collapse, prompting a short-term equity rally and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative when INDPRO falls and UNRATE stays below 5.0%, or the regime-specific signal fails to achieve a positive Rank IC over a 2-year rolling window.
